phase1_details.reason: Both are podcast producers tracking episode performance (downloads) and ad reads with the goal of producing a recurring performance summary/dashboard for team decision-making.
All focus on defining/standardizing KPI metric definitions/formulas (e.g., ARR/MRR, gross margin, EBITDA) so analyses and reporting use consistent calculations.
Both involve time-series sensor/experiment data with missing readings due to gaps and the goal of filling/interpolating those missing values.
All involve time-stamped activity/usage readings with missing hourly/daily values and the goal of imputing/interpolating missing measurements while ensuring timestamps are handled correctly.
Both are about filling missing values by carrying forward/forward-filling the most recent known value in a dataset (amounts/department codes).
Both scenarios involve analyzing sales data for e-commerce businesses, focusing on identifying trends.
Both scenarios involve preparing reports for corporate leadership with visual highlights for key metrics.
Both scenarios involve tracking financial transactions (commissions/payments) to analyze earnings or reconcile data.
Both scenarios involve small business inventory or sales tracking against operational data.
Both scenarios involve analyzing sales trends for business planning purposes.
Both scenarios involve analyzing digital marketing campaign performance to optimize spend.
Both scenarios involve standardizing inconsistent contact data formats before integration into a system.
Both scenarios involve data type standardization (OS names/price columns) for better analytics.
Both scenarios involve filtering or analyzing specialized datasets (patient records/star luminosity) for research purposes.
Both scenarios involve restructuring data from wide to long format for analysis or reporting.
Both scenarios involve tracking costs (print/class registrations) and analyzing trends over time.
Both scenarios involve personal fitness tracking (heart rate/workouts) with a focus on data validation or presentation.
Both scenarios involve hobbyist tracking (espresso shots/pantry inventory) for optimization or management.
Both scenarios involve cleaning or merging datasets to remove duplicates or standardize entries.
Both scenarios involve marketing professionals preparing performance reports with analytical insights.

Both involve exporting cleaned data for statistical analysis in SPSS/Excel for research purposes — same domain (research data export) and same user goal (saving cleaned dataset for downstream statistical analysis).

All three involve validating that categorical/code values in a dataset conform to an approved list before submitting a report to a compliance or finance authority — same domain (data validation for compliance reporting) and same user goal.

Both involve validating that field values in a dataset conform to an approved set of allowed options before exporting/submitting — dana_051 (voter party affiliation codes) and dana_337 (vendor categories and power requirements) share the same core goal of categorical value validation, though domains differ slightly. However, dana_051 is more specifically about inconsistent historical data entry while dana_337 is about pre-export validation — these are close but not strong duplicates.

Both involve a municipal transit authority analyst merging or comparing ridership data across time periods (before/after or year-over-year) to assess changes by route/line — same domain and same analytical goal.
Both involve preparing a recurring performance dashboard/report for investors at a startup — same domain (startup investor reporting) and same user goal (churn/performance metrics for investors).

Both involve filling down missing repeated values across line items in sales logs and then summarizing trends by category — same data cleaning goal (fill-down) and same analytical goal (sales summary), just different hobby/business contexts.

Both involve a hobbyist removing duplicate entries from repeated imports and standardizing date/format fields in a personal tracking log — same domain (hobbyist log deduplication and formatting) and same user goal.
Both involve splitting full names into first/given and last/surname columns for sorting/integration.
Both are about removing duplicate records from a startup dataset to improve data accuracy (customer feedback/user data deduplication).
Both scenarios involve analyzing standardized test scores for educational reporting, focusing on district-level data.
Both scenarios involve analyzing server logs with a focus on timestamp conversion for error tracking.
